description:
GITI-033 merge-blocker. An nested inside a ternary-markup consequent (${ @show ?
    …
: "" }) — iterating a module/cell source with NO enclosing for-loop — must lower like the identical each OUTSIDE the ternary. codes-half: E-CODEGEN-INVALID-LOGIC does NOT fire on the valid source. runtime-half: the list renders, the per-item TEXT body (${@.kind} / ${@.path}) interpolates the item values, and the per-item ATTRIBUTE (class="tag tag-${@.kind}") lowers to the iter binding.
rationale: §17.7.3 makes `@.` legal in ANY markup context (incl. a ternary-markup consequent); pre-fix the null-enclosing-scope gate dropped the lowering and the `@.` leaked raw → E-CODEGEN-INVALID-LOGIC. Anchored mode is impl-neutral (the each-container <div> wrapper is impl-private); .row count + tag/path text + the tag-<kind> class prove the body AND attribute interpolations render.
